scenario: A company uses Microsoft Dataverse plug-ins to perform complex logic. A synchronous plug-in that performs many data operations generates a timeout. The plugin step is registered for the update message on a table with the PostOperation stage selected. You need to improve the performance of the plug-in.
options: Use multi-threads to perform the data operations.
Register pre and post entity images.
Use batch requests to perform the data operations.
answer: Register pre and post entity images.
why: Register pre and post entity images so the plug-in can read the column values it needs directly from the image snapshots (context.PreEntityImages / PostEntityImages) instead of issuing extra Retrieve calls to the database. Removing those round-trips reduces the data operations and speeds up the plug-in, avoiding the timeout. Multi-threading is not supported/safe in the sandboxed plug-in pipeline, shared variables only pass data between plug-in stages, and batch requests apply to the Web API - none of them cut the redundant retrieves the way entity images do.
